title: [P0] Foreign key trap in POST /judge — invalid user_uuid causes 500
description: If a client sends a user_uuid that doesn't exist in users table, JudgeRun insert fails with integrity error wrapped in a 500 with raw SQLAlchemy error. Real user-facing bug: clearing localStorage + re-signup gives new UUID, old cached UUID is now invalid.
close_reason: Fixed: POST /judge now validates user_uuid exists before DB operations. Returns 404 for unknown UUIDs instead of 500 FK violation. Frontend auto-signs-out on 404 to force re-login.

title: [v0.0.1] SQLite local dev support
description:
Spec: docs/v0.0.1-pre/README.md (Backend req #6)

Make the backend runnable locally without PostgreSQL by defaulting to SQLite.

- [ ] config.py: default DATABASE_URL to sqlite:///./judge_agent.db, add is_sqlite property
- [ ] db/dbos.py: skip pool params for SQLite, add check_same_thread=False
- [ ] main.py: auto-create tables on startup in development+sqlite mode
- [ ] alembic/env.py: render_as_batch=True for SQLite ALTER TABLE support
- [ ] pyproject.toml: move psycopg2-binary to optional postgres extra
- [ ] .env.example + .env: default to SQLite URL
close_reason: Implemented and pushed. SQLite is now the default for local dev.

title: [P0] DBOS wired but may not work — no dbos-config.yaml, no docs
description: main.py initializes DBOS with DBOS_SYSTEM_DATABASE_URL. No dbos-config.yaml in repo. DBOS library requires config file — without it, workflow decorator is either no-op or crash. Neither outcome documented. Durability story may be theater.
close_reason: DBOS v2.13.0 supports programmatic DBOSConfig dict — no dbos-config.yaml needed. Verified: DBOS launches successfully with SQLite system DB. The admin server port conflict warning is benign (only appears when another instance runs).

title: [v0.0.8] Fixtures CLI (Internal Tool)

Spec: docs/v0.0.8-pre/README.md

Internal CLI: dump positive feedback into eval fixtures. Only rating=up entries become fixtures. Idempotent by text content.

Acceptance Criteria:

- [ ] Fixtures appended to correct file based on origin.prediction
- [ ] Duplicate entries not added (idempotent)
- [ ] Summary printed to stdout

- [ ] No --user flag dumps all users

title: [bug] Race condition on signup can 500 under concurrent same-username requests
description:
backend/app/api/auth.py:27-33

Read-then-insert without conflict handling. Two concurrent requests for the same username can both see 'no user exists', then one INSERT fails on the unique constraint with an unhandled IntegrityError → 500.

Fix: catch IntegrityError, rollback, re-query existing user.
close_reason: Wrapped insert in try/except IntegrityError; rollback + re-query on collision

title: [bug] lifespan startup init runs once per uvicorn worker, not once per process group
description:
backend/app/main.py:25-33

lifespan() runs in each worker process. With multiple uvicorn workers, DBOS.launch() and create_all_tables() execute N times (once per worker). create_all_tables() is idempotent so no correctness bug today, but repeated DBOS init adds startup overhead and could cause issues if DBOS ever enforces single-init semantics.

Fix: guard with an env var or process-level flag, or note it as accepted risk and document the constraint.
close_reason: Added sentinel file to skip repeated create_all_tables() DDL on subsequent workers; documented DBOS per-worker behavior with comment

title: [bug] Upload endpoint reads entire files into memory
description:
backend/app/api/upload.py:39-50

await video_file.read() and await subtitle_file.read() load full payloads into RAM before writing to disk. Large video uploads can spike per-worker memory and OOM the process.

Fix: stream in chunks using shutil.copyfileobj or manual async chunk loop.
close_reason: Replaced full read() with aiofiles 1MiB chunk streaming; added aiofiles to pyproject.toml dependencies

title: [v0.0.6] Video Pipeline: Transcription + Frame Extraction
description:
Spec: docs/v0.0.6-pre/README.md

Core video pipeline. Two DBOS workflows run in parallel after upload: (A) transcription via ElevenLabs Scribe or subtitle parsing, (B) frame extraction via ffmpeg-python. Then (C) judge with combined transcript+frames.

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] Upload .mp4 → both transcription and frame extraction run
- [ ] Upload .mp4 + .srt → transcription skipped, frames still extracted
- [ ] ./tmp/{upload_id}/transcript.txt exists after pipeline
- [ ] ./tmp/{upload_id}/frames/ contains extracted JPGs
- [ ] DBOS retries step A independently if ElevenLabs fails
- [ ] DBOS retries step B independently if ffmpeg fails
- [ ] POST /judge/video response is valid JudgeOutput
- [ ] ffmpeg-python>=0.2.0 in pyproject.toml
close_reason: Video pipeline implemented: transcription, frame extraction, DBOS workflow, POST /judge/video

title: [bug] Race condition on judge result caching can 500 on duplicate run_id
description:
backend/app/api/judge.py:41-60

Same read-then-insert pattern: deterministic run_id (md5 of content+user_uuid) means concurrent identical requests both pass the 'existing' check, then one INSERT fails on PK collision with an unhandled IntegrityError → 500.

Fix: catch IntegrityError, rollback, re-query and return existing row.
close_reason: Wrapped cache insert in try/except IntegrityError; rollback + re-query on PK collision
